title: pneumonanthe vulgaris
intro: Pneumonanthe vulgaris, also known as lungwort, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its unique appearance of mottled green and white leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of lungwort include improved lung function, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune system response, making it a popular choice in traditional herbal medicine. Lungwort is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be consumed as a supplement in capsule or powder form.
